title: Establish Driver's License Reinstatement Fee Debt Reduction Prog.
description: To enact sections 4510.101, 4510.102, 4510.103, 4510.104, 4510.105, 4510.106, 4510.107, and 4510.108 of the Revised Code to establish a permanent Driver's License Reinstatement Fee Debt Reduction and Amnesty Program.

title: Change Freemason license plate
description: To amend sections 4501.21 and 4503.70 of the Revised Code to make changes to the "Freemason" license plate.

title: Regards Medicaid waivers for relatives of active duty military
description: To enact section 5166.09 of the Revised Code regarding Medicaid home and community-based waiver services for relatives of active duty military.

title: Amend eminent domain laws
description: To enact section 163.022 of the Revised Code to amend the law regarding eminent domain and to declare an emergency.

title: Revise elevator laws
Revoke professional licenses for criminal misconduct
description: To amend sections 2907.01, 2907.05, 2907.17, 2907.18, 2907.24, 4715.30, 4729.16, 4730.25, 4731.22, 4732.17, 4732.171, 4755.11, 4755.47, and 4755.64, to enact sections 4725.241 and 4753.102 of the Revised Code, and to amend the version of section 2907.05 of the Revised Code that is scheduled to take effect March 22, 2020, to prohibit unlawful sexual contact between health care professionals and patients and to revoke professional licenses for criminal misconduct.

title: Privilege communications-first responder peer recovery supporters
description: To amend section 2317.02 of the Revised Code to provide privileged testimonial communications between a peer recovery supporter and a fellow first responder exhibiting signs of distress.

title: Provide universal health care coverage
description: To amend section 109.02 and to enact sections 3920.01, 3920.02, 3920.03, 3920.04, 3920.05, 3920.06, 3920.07, 3920.08, 3920.09, 3920.10, 3920.11, 3920.12, 3920.13, 3920.14, 3920.15, 3920.21, 3920.22, 3920.23, 3920.24, 3920.25, 3920.26, 3920.27, 3920.28, 3920.31, 3920.32, and 3920.33 of the Revised Code to establish and operate the Ohio Health Care Plan to provide universal health care coverage to all Ohio residents.

title: Designate Patrolman David L. Sterner Memorial Overpass
description: To enact section 5534.421 of the Revised Code to designate the overpass on State Route 37 that spans State Route 15 in Hancock County as the "Patrolman David L. Sterner Memorial Overpass."

title: Modify school bullying, intimidation, harassment definitions
description: To amend section 3313.666 of the Revised Code to modify the definition of school harassment, intimidation, or bullying.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Establish requirements for low-speed electric scooters
description: To amend sections 1345.022, 4501.01, 4509.01, 4511.01, 4511.513, 4511.522, 4511.68, and 4511.711 and to enact section 4511.514 of the Revised Code to amend the law related to unsafe tires, establish requirements governing low-speed micromobility devices, and to make other changes to the law related to special modes of transportation.

title: Create Healthy New Albany license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.931 of the Revised Code to create the "Healthy New Albany" license plate.

title: Grant tax credit-contribute cash to pregnancy resource center
description: To amend sections 5747.08 and 5747.98 and to enact section 5747.77 of the Revised Code to authorize a nonrefundable income tax credit for a taxpayer's cash contributions to qualifying pregnancy resource centers.

title: Modify statute of limitations for child abuse and neglect
description: To amend section 2901.13 of the Revised Code to modify the period of limitation for prosecution of offenses involving child abuse and neglect.

title: Enhance penalty for crimes against correction and parole officers
description: To amend sections 2903.01, 2903.11, and 2923.02 of the Revised Code to enhance penalties for certain crimes against corrections officers, parole officers, probation officers, and parole board members.

title: Create Rabbit Friendly license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.507 of the Revised Code to create the "Rabbit Friendly" license plate.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Establish process-obtain title to aircraft left on property
description: To enact section 4561.26 of the Revised Code to establish a process by which a person may obtain title to an aircraft that has been left on the person's property.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Include child abuse-related offenses in violent offender database
description: To amend section 2903.41 of the Revised Code to include certain child abuse-related offenses in the violent offender database, to name the act Jacob's Law, and to make an appropriation.

title: Designate PFC Steven D. Smith Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.899 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 13 in Richland County as the "PFC Steven D. Smith Memorial Highway."

title: Enact Equal Pay Act
description: To amend sections 4112.04 and 4117.08 and to enact sections 9.79, 9.791, 9.792, 9.793, 9.794, 9.795, 9.796, 9.797, 9.798, 142.01, 142.02, 142.03, 142.04, 142.05, 142.06, 142.07, 142.08, 142.09, 142.10, 4113.12, 4113.42, and 4117.141 of the Revised Code to enact the Ohio Equal Pay Act to require state contractors and economic incentive recipients to obtain an equal pay certificate, to require public employers to establish a job evaluation system to identify and eliminate sex-based wage disparities, to prohibit employers from seeking a prospective employee's wage or salary history, and to prohibit employer retaliation against an employee who discusses salary or wage rates with another employee.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Create new school financing system
Create Habitat for Humanity of Ohio license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.931 of the Revised Code to create the "Habitat for Humanity of Ohio" license plate.

title: Increase penalties for voyeurism
description: To amend section 2907.08 of the Revised Code to increase the penalties for the offense of voyeurism.

title: Concerns workers' compensation and disability retirement for PTSD
description: To enact section 126.65 of the Revised Code to establish a fund to provide compensation and benefits to first responders with post-traumatic stress disorder and to study the financial and administrative requirements for that fund.

title: Increase penalties-assault of transit operator/evading fares
description: To amend sections 2903.13 and 2917.41 and to enact section 306.20 of the Revised Code to increase the penalty for assault when the victim is an operator of an Ohio transit system bus or rail car; to authorize Ohio transit systems to post a sign regarding abuse or assault of staff; and to increase the penalty for evading payment of the known fares of a public transportation system.

title: Enacts Anti-Bullying and Hazing Act
description: To amend sections 2903.31, 3301.22, 3313.661, 3313.666, 3314.03, and 3326.11 and to enact sections 2903.311, 3301.165, 3313.669, 3319.318, and 3345.19 of the Revised Code to enact Collin's Law: The "Ohio Anti-Bullying and Hazing Act" with regard to school discipline and bullying and hazing policies at schools and colleges.

title: Authorizes counties to obtain a county inspector general
description: To amend sections 102.06, 121.41, and 121.42 and to enact sections 121.54, 305.20, 318.01, 318.02, 318.03, 318.04, 318.05, 318.06, 318.07, 318.08, 318.081, 318.09, and 318.10 of the Revised Code to authorize counties to request and obtain a county inspector general to investigate wrongful acts or omissions in county and other political subdivision government and to allow counties to contract with the State Inspector General on a limited basis.
last_action: Reported - Substitute: State and Local Government

title: Permits certain intrastate equity crowdfunding
Create Ohio Daughters of American Revolution license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.766 of the Revised Code to create the "Ohio Society Daughters of the American Revolution" license plate.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Create Anti-Prostitution Grant Program Fund
description: To enact section 109.661 of the Revised Code to create the anti-prostitution grant program fund and to make an appropriation.

title: Require gun dealers give suicide prevention information
description: To amend sections 2923.25 and 5502.63 of the Revised Code to require federally licensed firearms dealers to provide suicide prevention information to the purchaser of a firearm.

title: Get court order temporarily restricting access to guns
description: To amend sections 109.57, 2923.125, 2923.128, and 2923.13 and to enact sections 2923.26, 2923.27, 2923.28, 2923.29, 2923.30, and 2923.99 of the Revised Code to enact the Extreme Risk Protection Order Act to allow family members, household members, and law enforcement officers to obtain a court order that temporarily restricts a person's access to firearms if that person poses a danger to themselves or others.

title: Limit how gun may be transferred and require background check
description: To amend sections 2929.28 and 5122.311 and to enact section 2923.26 of the Revised Code to enact the Protect Law Enforcement Act to require a firearm transfer to be made through a dealer, through a law enforcement agency, or pursuant to a specified exception, and to require a background check when a firearm is transferred.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Regards sales of kratom products
description: To amend sections 3715.01, 3715.99, 3717.01, and 3719.99 and to enact sections 3715.026 and 3717.34 of the Revised Code regarding sales of kratom products.

title: Restore local authority to regulate firearm-related conduct
description: To repeal section 9.68 of the Revised Code and to repeal on December 28, 2019, the version of section 9.68 of the Revised Code that is scheduled to take effect on that date, to restore local authority to generally regulate firearms-related conduct.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Prohibit dealer transferring firearm if background check pending
description: To amend section 2923.25 of the Revised Code to prohibit a federally licensed firearms dealer from transferring a firearm while a background check is pending unless 30 days have elapsed.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Regards age-appropriate child sex abuse instruction
description: To amend sections 3313.60 and 3319.073 and to enact sections 3314.0310 and 3326.091 of the Revised Code with respect to age-appropriate student instruction in child sexual abuse and sexual violence prevention and in-service staff training in child sexual abuse prevention.

title: Revise Ohio Teacher Residency Program
description: To amend sections 3319.111, 3319.22, 3319.223, 3319.227, 3319.229, and 3319.26 of the Revised Code to revise the Ohio Teacher Residency Program.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Authorize psychologists to prescribe drugs/therapeutic devices
Raise limits on noneconomic and punitive damages in tort actions
description: To amend sections 2307.80, 2315.18, 2315.21, 2323.43, and 3345.40 of the Revised Code to raise the limitations on damages for noneconomic loss and punitive damages in certain tort actions based on adjustments for inflation from the year 2005 to 2019.

title: Designate Toni Morrison Day-February 18
description: To enact section 5.293 of the Revised Code to designate February 18 as "Toni Morrison Day."

title: PERS/SERS disability benefits continue if elected to office
description: To amend sections 145.362 and 3309.41 and to enact section 145.364 of the Revised Code to allow a Public Employees Retirement System or School Employees Retirement System disability benefit recipient elected to certain offices to continue receiving a disability benefit during the term of office.

title: Prohibit business from using a drone to deliver packages
description: To enact section 4511.515 of the Revised Code to prohibit a business from using an unmanned aerial vehicle to deliver packages.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Prohibit class B firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals
description: To enact section 3737.52 of the Revised Code to prohibit the use of class B firefighting foam containing intentionally added PFAS chemicals for testing and training purposes.

title: Prohibit sun lamp tanning services for those under 18
description: To amend sections 4713.50, 4713.51, and 4713.64 of the Revised Code to prohibit the provision of sun lamp tanning services to individuals under age 18.

title: Regards workers' comp experience in firefighter cancer claims
description: To amend section 4123.68 of the Revised Code regarding charging workers' compensation experience in firefighter cancer claims.

title: Require campaign treasurer statement acknowledging appointment
description: To amend section 3517.10 of the Revised Code to require an entity to include with the form to be filed with the Secretary of State when appointing a campaign treasurer a notarized signature of that treasurer acknowledging the appointment and to amend the version of section 3517.10 of the Revised Code that is scheduled to take effect January 1, 2021, to continue the provisions of this act on and after that effective date.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Designate Purple Heart Trail of Defiance Co.; Purple Heart Bridge
description: To enact sections 5534.821 and 5534.822 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 66 in Defiance County as the "Purple Heart Trail of Defiance County" and to designate the bridge spanning the Maumee River that is part of State Route 66, in the City of Defiance, as the "Purple Heart Bridge."

title: Permit schools to contract for digital student record mgmt
description: To amend sections 3314.03, 3326.11, and 3328.24 and to enact section 3301.943 of the Revised Code to permit public schools to enter into agreements with third parties to provide digital student record storage, management, and retrieval.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Designates Cpl. Fredrick Wayne Miller Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.829 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 39 in Holmes County as the "Cpl. Fredrick Wayne Miller Memorial Highway."

title: Require subject of certain protection orders surrender firearms
description: To amend sections 2919.26 and 3113.31 and to enact sections 2919.261 and 3113.311 of the Revised Code to require a person who is subject to a civil or criminal domestic violence temporary protection order to surrender the person's firearms.

title: Reinstates the Joint Education Oversight Committee
description: To enact sections 103.44, 103.45, 103.46, 103.47, 103.48, 103.49, and 103.50 of the Revised Code and to amend Section 311.10 of Am. Sub. H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly and to repeal Section 733.40 of Am. Sub. H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to reinstate the Joint Education Oversight Committee, make an appropriation, and declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Provides privileged communications for victim advocacy services
description: To amend sections 2151.421, 2317.02, and 2921.22 of the Revised Code to provide generally a privilege for communications between a qualified advocate rendering advocacy services and a victim of certain crimes, to exempt the nondisclosure of that privileged communication from the offense of failure to report a crime, and to generally require a qualified advocate to report child abuse or neglect of the victim.

title: Enacts Mental Health Awareness and Comm. Violence Protection Act
description: To amend sections 2151.34, 2903.213, 2903.214, 2919.26, 2923.13, 2923.14, 3113.31, 3113.99, and 5122.10 and to enact sections 3113.26, 3113.261, 3113.27, 3113.28, 3113.29, and 3113.30 of the Revised Code to enact the Mental Health Awareness and Community Violence Protection Act.

title: Enacts the Insurance Code Correction Act
Revises Ohio's drainage laws
Regards administering addiction treatment drugs
description: To amend sections 121.22, 2925.01, 2925.61, 4723.50, 4723.52, 4729.01, 4729.29, 4729.44, 4729.45, 4729.51, 4729.514, 4729.541, 4729.553, 4729.80, 4730.56, and 4731.83; to amend, for the purpose of adopting new section numbers as indicated in parentheses, sections 4723.486 (4723.488), 4723.488 (4723.484), and 4730.431 (4730.434); and to enact new section 4723.486 and sections 4723.485, 4729.515, 4730.435, and 4730.436 of the Revised Code regarding the administration of addiction treatment drugs, federal agency access to the Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System, the Board of Pharmacy's exemption from open meetings requirements, the occasional sale of certain drugs at wholesale, and naloxone access and education.

title: Designate Adams County Vietnam Veterans Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.819 of the Revised Code to designate U.S. Route 52 in Adams County the "Adams County Vietnam Veterans Memorial Highway."

title: Appropriate money-emergency water and sewer system funding
description: To make an appropriation related to emergency water and sewer system funding.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Create Cleveland Clinic Children's license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.899 of the Revised Code to create the "Cleveland Clinic Children's" license plate.

title: Obtain title to motor vehicle ordered into storage by police
description: To amend section 4513.62 and to enact section 4505.104 of the Revised Code to allow the owner of a towing service or storage facility to obtain certificates of title to unclaimed motor vehicles ordered into storage by law enforcement.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation, Commerce and Workforce

title: Prohibit sale of flavored smoking/vaping devices/study risks
description: To amend section 2927.02 of the Revised Code to prohibit the sale of flavored electronic smoking devices and flavored vapor products and to establish a committee to study the health risks associated with such products.

title: Restrict vapor product retailers
description: To amend section 2927.02 of the Revised Code to place restrictions on retailers of vapor products.

title: Prohibit person under protection order buying/receiving firearm
description: To amend sections 2903.214, 2919.26, and 3113.31 and to enact section 2923.22 of the Revised Code to prohibit a person subject to a protection order from purchasing or receiving a firearm for the duration of the order.

title: Prohibit possession of large capacity magazine
description: To amend section 109.52 and to enact sections 109.5731, 2923.191, and 2923.192 of the Revised Code to generally prohibit a person from possessing a large capacity magazine and to establish a large capacity magazine registry.

title: Re-enact animal fighting and bestiality laws
description: To amend sections 959.15, 959.21, 959.99, and 1717.06 of the Revised Code to re-enact provisions of law governing animal fighting, bestiality, and residency requirements for humane society agents.

title: Terminate lease if victim of specified crime
description: To amend sections 3767.01, 3767.05, 4301.74, and 5321.02 and to enact section 5321.172 of the Revised Code to allow a tenant to terminate a rental agreement if the tenant is a victim of a specified crime.

title: Modify civil rights laws relating to employment
description: To amend sections 2315.18, 2315.21, 4112.01, 4112.02, 4112.04, 4112.05, 4112.08, 4112.14, and 4112.99; to amend, for the purpose of adopting new section numbers as indicated in parentheses, sections 4112.051 (4112.055) and 4112.052 (4112.056); and to enact new sections 4112.051 and 4112.052 and section 4112.054 of the Revised Code to modify Ohio civil rights laws related to employment.

title: Designate Clarence "Sonny" Smith, Jr., Highway
description: To enact section 5534.921 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 7 in Mahoning County as the "Clarence "Sonny" Smith, Jr. Highway."

title: To improve background checks, close gaps between federal and state law, improve focus on mental health disorders and to make an appropriation.
description: To amend sections 2151.358, 2903.214, 2919.26, 2923.13, 2923.14, 2929.44, 2945.402, 3113.31, and 5122.01 and to enact sections 5502.80, 5502.81, and 5502.82 of the Revised Code to require the juvenile court to expunge all records sealed pursuant to the juvenile sealing law upon the person's twenty-eighth birthday, to expand the circumstances under which a person has a weapon under disability, to specify that moderate or severe substance use disorder is a mental illness for purposes of the law governing civil commitments, to require the Director of Public Safety to create and maintain the weapons disability data portal, to impose certain consequences on specified entities that fail to comply with data submission requirements, and to make an appropriation.

title: Provides immunity to camp operators for inherent camping risks
description: To enact section 3729.15 of the Revised Code to provide a qualified immunity to camp operators for harm arising from a risk inherent to camping.

title: Regards Cleveland Housing Court's jurisdiction for local codes
description: To amend section 1901.181 of the Revised Code to grant the Cleveland Housing Court jurisdiction in any review or appeal of a final order of an administrative body that relates to a local building, health, or safety code.

title: Give Cleveland Housing Court environmental pollution jurisdiction
description: To amend section 1901.181 of the Revised Code to grant the Housing Division of the Cleveland Municipal Court concurrent jurisdiction with the Court of Common Pleas in criminal cases related to environmental pollution.

title: Designate Eugene 'Gene' F. Kranz Day
description: To enact sections 5.2316, 5.2521, 5.2522, 5.2525, and 5.293 of the Revised Code to make multiple special designations.
committee: Senate Health, Human Services and Medicaid
last_action: Refer to Committee: Health, Human Services and Medicaid

title: Allow WWII vets obtain WWII license plates without taxes and fees
description: To amend section 4503.29 of the Revised Code to allow veterans of World War II to obtain World War II license plates without paying motor vehicle registration taxes and fees.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Require water bottle stations and drinking fountains in schools
description: To enact sections 3318.038 and 3781.1011 of the Revised Code to require water bottle filling stations and drinking fountains in certain public school buildings and to establish requirements for battery-charged fences in nonresidential properties for the state and for political subdivisions.

title: Designate Ohio Tuskegee Airmen Day
description: To enact section 5.246 of the Revised Code to designate March 29 as "Ohio Tuskegee Airmen Day."

title: Create the crime of masked intimidation
description: To enact section 2917.14 of the Revised Code to create the crime of masked intimidation, a misdemeanor of the first degree.

title: Designate Coming Out Day
description: To enact section 5.246 of the Revised Code to designate October 11 as "Coming Out Day."
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Designate Aortic Aneurysm Awareness Day
description: To enact section 5.2521 of the Revised Code to designate February 13 as "Aortic Aneurysm Awareness Day."

title: Revise requirements for chemical dependency counselor II license
description: To amend sections 2925.01, 2925.03, 4758.20, 4758.42, 4758.61, 5164.751, and 5167.01 and to enact sections 3902.50, 3902.51, 4729.49, and 5167.123 of the Revised Code to revise the requirements for a chemical dependency counselor II license, to prohibit a health plan issuer or Medicaid managed care organization from taking certain actions with respect to reimbursements to 340B covered entities, and to enhance penalties for certain drug trafficking offenses committed in the vicinity of a substance addiction services provider and to name the act's drug trafficking provisions the "Relapse Reduction Act."
last_action: Refused to concur in Senate amendments

title: Revise prohibition against criminal child enticement
description: To amend section 2905.05 of the Revised Code to revise the general prohibition against criminal child enticement.

title: Recommend school counselor job description/designate liaisons
description: To enact section 3319.091 of the Revised Code to require the Department of Education to make recommendations for a job description for school counselors and to designate at least one staff member as a school counselor liaison.

title: Enact Ohio Computer Crimes Act
Expand civil rights laws-sexual orientation/remedies/religious
description: To amend sections 9.03, 124.93, 340.12, 511.03, 717.01, 1501.012, 1751.18, 2927.03, 3113.36, 3301.53, 3304.15, 3304.50, 3314.06, 3332.09, 3721.13, 3905.55, 4111.17, 4112.01, 4112.02, 4112.021, 4112.04, 4112.05, 4112.08, 4117.19, 4725.67, 4735.16, 4735.55, 4744.54, 4757.07, 4758.16, 4765.18, 5104.09, 5107.26, 5123.351, 5126.07, 5165.08, 5312.04, 5515.08, and 5709.832 of the Revised Code to enact the Ohio Fairness Act to prohibit discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity or expression, to add mediation as an informal method that the Ohio Civil Rights Commission may use, and to uphold existing religious exemptions under Ohio's Civil Rights Law.

title: Allow AG to defend subdivisions in First Amendment cases
description: To enact section 109.141 of the Revised Code to allow the Attorney General to defend political subdivisions in certain actions brought under the First Amendment to the United States Constitution or analogous provisions of the Ohio Constitution.

title: Grant in-state tuition if receive high school equivalence in Ohio
description: To amend section 3333.31 of the Revised Code to expand the "Forever Buckeye" program by granting in-state college tuition to individuals who receive a certificate of high school equivalence in Ohio.

title: Renew driver's license/ID cards every eight years
description: To amend sections 4506.08, 4506.14, 4507.09, 4507.23, 4507.24, 4507.50, 4507.52, and 4511.521 of the Revised Code to authorize a person to renew specified driver's licenses or state identification cards every eight years beginning July 1, 2021.

title: Eliminate apprentice/special auctioneer licenses/other changes
description: To amend sections 4707.01, 4707.02, 4707.021, 4707.04, 4707.05, 4707.06, 4707.07, 4707.073, 4707.08, 4707.091, 4707.10, 4707.11, 4707.12, 4707.14, 4707.15, 4707.151, 4707.16, 4707.171, 4707.18, 4707.19, 4707.20, 4707.21, 4707.22, and 4707.25 and to repeal sections 4707.071 and 4707.09 of the Revised Code to eliminate the apprentice auctioneer and special auctioneer's license and to make other revisions to the law governing auctions.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Agriculture and Rural Development

title: Revise massage therapy law
description: To amend sections 503.40, 503.41, 503.42, 503.43, 503.44, 503.47, 503.48, 503.49, 503.50, 715.61, 2927.17, 4731.04, 4731.15, and 4731.41; to enact section 503.411; and to repeal sections 503.45 and 503.46 of the Revised Code to make changes to the massage therapy licensing law.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Government Oversight and Reform

title: Grant income tax credit-extracurricular activity fees
description: To amend sections 5747.08 and 5747.98 and to enact section 5747.77 of the Revised Code to authorize a refundable income tax credit for individuals who pay an extracurricular activity fee on behalf of a student from a low-income family.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Ways and Means

title: Designate CPL Benjamin C. Dillon Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.828 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of Interstate Route 76 in Portage County the "CPL Benjamin C. Dillon Memorial Highway."

title: Designate William "Bill" Burgett Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.914 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 13 in Knox County as the "William 'Bill' Burgett Memorial Highway."

title: Provide unemployment benefits to strikers
description: To amend section 4141.29 and to enact section 4141.294 of the Revised Code to provide unemployment benefits to striking workers and to declare an emergency.

title: Designate Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) Day
description: To enact section 5.2521 of the Revised Code to designate April 26 as "Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) Day."

title: Require owners to pay construction contractors within 35 days
description: To amend section 4113.61 of the Revised Code to require owners of construction projects to pay a contractor within thirty-five days of receiving a request for payment.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Enact modifications to law regarding self-defense
description: To amend sections 2307.601, 2901.05, and 2901.09 and to enact sections 2901.091 and 2901.092 of the Revised Code to enact the Ohio Stand Your Ground Act to modify the law regarding self-defense.

title: Prohibit municipal income tax-certain nonresident income
description: To amend sections 709.023, 718.01, 718.02, 718.03, 718.04, 718.05, 718.16, 718.82, and 5703.94 and to repeal sections 718.011 and 718.50 of the Revised Code to prohibit municipal corporations from levying an income tax on nonresidents' compensation for personal services or on net profits from a sole proprietorship owned by a nonresident.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Ways and Means

title: Designate Bleeding Disorders Awareness Month
description: To enact section 5.2522 of the Revised Code to designate March as "Bleeding Disorders Awareness Month."

title: Request by residents for stop sign or lower speed limit
description: To amend sections 4511.11 and 4511.21 of the Revised Code to permit a local resident, neighborhood association, or neighborhood organization to request the erection of a stop sign at an intersection and to support a request for a lower prima-facie speed limit on certain streets and highways.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Investigate insulin pricing
description: To require the Attorney General to investigate insulin pricing and prepare and submit a report.

title: Establish Commercial Truck Driver Student Aid Program
description: To amend sections 3333.38 and 3345.32 and to enact section 3333.125 of the Revised Code and to amend Sections 381.10 and 381.340 of Am. Sub. H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to establish the Commercial Truck Driver Student Aid program and to make an appropriation.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Economic and Workforce Development

title: Cap cost-sharing for prescription insulin drugs
description: To enact section 3902.50 of the Revised Code to cap cost-sharing for prescription insulin drugs.

title: Regards out-of-network health care
description: To enact sections 3902.50, 3902.51, 3902.52, 3902.53, and 3902.54 of the Revised Code regarding out-of-network care.

title: Allow nonprofit organization to give away alcohol to raise funds
description: To amend section 4301.58 of the Revised Code to allow a nonprofit organization to give away alcohol for fundraising purposes under specified circumstances and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Regards health insurance premiums and benefits
Increases state minimum wage
description: To amend sections 4111.02, 4111.09, and 4111.14 and to repeal section 4111.07 of the Revised Code to increase the state minimum wage to ten dollars and fifty-five cents per hour beginning January 1, 2021.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Designates Lester and Mary Oeder Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.915 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of U.S. Route 22 in Warren County the "Lester and Mary Oeder Memorial Highway."

title: Make apple cider official state beverage
description: To amend section 5.08 of the Revised Code to make apple cider instead of tomato juice the official beverage of the state.

title: Designate Plastic Pollution Awareness Day
description: To enact section 5.22101 of the Revised Code to designate the fifteenth day of February as "Plastic Pollution Awareness Day."
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Modify law regarding physician assistants
Stay in same school district if move after first day
description: To amend section 3313.64 of the Revised Code to permit a child whose parents live in different school districts to continue to attend school in the school district in which the child attended school at the beginning of the school year even if the child moves to a different school district.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Establish loan repayment program for eligible teachers
description: To enact section 3319.58 of the Revised Code to establish a loan repayment program for eligible teachers.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Revise farm bus motor vehicle registration period
description: To amend section 4503.04 of the Revised Code to increase the motor vehicle registration period for a farm bus.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Grant apiary owners immunity for bee stings
description: To enact section 909.19 of the Revised Code to grant specified apiary owners immunity in personal injury or property damage cases involving bee stings.

title: Require EPA Director to adopt rules for water quality
description: To amend section 6111.041 and to enact section 6109.26 of the Revised Code to require the Director of Environmental Protection to adopt rules establishing maximum allowable contaminant levels in drinking water and water quality standards for certain contaminants.

title: Authorize tax credit for disability-related home expenses
description: To amend sections 5747.08 and 5747.98 and to enact section 5747.85 of the Revised Code to authorize a refundable income tax credit for disability-related home expenses.

title: Implement motor fuel quality testing program
description: To amend section 1327.70 of the Revised Code to authorize a county to implement a motor fuel quality testing program.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Petition for visitation with incompetent or ward if denied
description: To enact sections 2111.53, 2111.532, 2111.533, 2111.535, 2111.536, 2111.537, 2111.539, 2111.5311, 2111.5313, and 2111.5314 of the Revised Code to allow certain persons to petition for visitation with an incompetent or ward if visitation has been interfered with or denied.

title: Clarify slow-moving vehicle law/animal-drawn vehicle lighting
description: To amend sections 4511.216, 4513.11, and 5733.98; to enact new section 4513.111 and sections 4513.112, 4513.113, 4513.114, and 4513.115; and to repeal sections 4513.111 and 5733.44 of the Revised Code to clarify the law governing slow-moving vehicles and to revise the lighting and reflective material requirements applicable to animal-drawn vehicles.

title: Adopt jail reforms relating to Minimum Standards for Jails
description: To amend section 5120.10 and to enact section 5120.101 of the Revised Code to adopt several jail reform procedures related to compliance with the Minimum Standards for Jails in Ohio.

title: Prohibit conversion therapy when treating minor patients
description: To enact sections 4723.93, 4731.92, 4732.34, 4743.09, 4757.46, and 4758.73 of the Revised Code to protect minors by prohibiting certain licensed health care professionals from engaging in conversion therapy when treating minor patients.

title: Prescribe per pupil funding guarantee for certain schools
description: To enact section 3317.28 of the Revised Code and to amend Sections 265.10 as subsequently amended, 265.210 as subsequently amended, and 265.220 of H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to prescribe a per pupil funding guarantee for certain school districts and to make an appropriation.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Eliminate authority of residential facilities in R-zones
description: To amend sections 5119.34, 5123.19, and 5123.196 and to repeal section 5119.341 of the Revised Code to eliminate the specific authority of residential facilities to operate within residential zoning districts.

title: Prohibit placement of child with relative child does not know
description: To enact section 2151.3511 of the Revised Code to prohibit, unless determined to be in a child's best interest, placement of the child with a relative the child does not know or has never met.

title: Regards enforcement of tax liens against owner-occupied homestead
description: To amend sections 317.22, 319.20, 323.25, 4503.06, 5721.18, and 5721.31 and to enact section 319.204 of the Revised Code to prohibit enforcement of delinquent property tax liens against owner-occupied homesteads and to require that any delinquent tax be paid before the title to a homestead may be transferred.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Ways and Means

title: Permit unemployment comp eligibility re: domestic violence
description: To amend section 4141.29 and to enact section 4141.294 of the Revised Code to allow an individual to be eligible for unemployment compensation benefits for unemployment due to domestic violence.

title: Regards licensure inspection for residential care facilities
description: To amend section 3721.02 of the Revised Code regarding expedited licensure inspection procedures for residential care facilities.
committee: Senate Health, Human Services and Medicaid
last_action: Refer to Committee: Health, Human Services and Medicaid

title: Increase penalty for failing to secure vehicle load
description: To amend sections 4513.31 and 4513.99 of the Revised Code to increase the criminal penalties associated with failing to secure a load on a vehicle.

title: Permit schools and camps to administer glucagon
description: To amend sections 3313.713, 4723.50, 4729.01, 4729.51, 4729.513, 4729.541, 4729.60, and 4729.88 and to enact sections 3313.7115, 3313.7116, 3314.147, 3326.60, 3328.38, 4723.484, 4730.434, 4731.92, and 5101.78 of the Revised Code to permit schools and camps to procure and use injectable or nasally administered glucagon in accordance with prescribed policies and to exempt them from licensing requirements related to the possession of glucagon.

title: Regards coverage of epinephrine and glucagon
description: To amend sections 5162.20 and 5167.12 and to enact sections 3902.50 and 5164.092 of the Revised Code regarding insurance and Medicaid coverage of epinephrine and glucagon.

title: Enacts the Vulnerable Child Protection Act
description: To enact sections 2151.63, 5128.01, 5128.02, 5128.03, 5128.04, 5128.05, 5128.06, and 5128.99 of the Revised Code to enact the "Vulnerable Child Protection Act" regarding sexuality and identity counseling and prohibiting certain procedures and activities intended to change, reinforce, or affirm a minor's perception of his or her own sexual attraction, sexual behaviors, or identity.

title: Require state retirement systems to broadcast board meetings
description: To amend sections 145.07, 742.07, 3307.09, 3309.09, and 5505.04 of the Revised Code to require the state retirement systems to publicly broadcast board meetings.

title: Require state retirement system boards to disclose certain info
description: To enact sections 145.117, 742.117, 3307.16, 3309.151, and 5505.066 of the Revised Code to require the boards of the state retirement systems to disclose certain financial information regarding alternative investments.

title: Regards state retirement system doing business with prior officer
description: To amend sections 145.112, 742.111, 3307.151, 3309.155, and 5505.08 of the Revised Code regarding the prohibition against the state retirement systems doing business with a former state retirement system employee, officer, or board member.

title: Explicitly exempt building and fire codes from rule review
description: To amend sections 3737.82 and 3781.10 of the Revised Code to explicitly exempt building and fire codes from certain rule review requirements.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Remove noneconomic damages cap for rape or assault victim
description: To amend section 2315.18 of the Revised Code to remove the cap on damages for noneconomic loss when a victim of rape or assault brings a tort action.

title: Designate Financial Literacy Month
description: To enact section 5.266 of the Revised Code to designate April as "Financial Literacy Month" in Ohio.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Establish pilot-assist applying-public service loan forgiveness
description: To amend Sections 381.10 and 381.20 of H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to establish a pilot program to assist public service employees in applying for the federal Public Service Loan Forgiveness Program and to make an appropriation.

title: Disseminate information about public service loan forgiveness
description: To enact section 3333.0417 of the Revised Code to require the Chancellor of Higher Education and public service organizations to disseminate information regarding the federal public service loan forgiveness programs.

title: Authorize conservancy districts-collect/dispose of solid waste
description: To amend sections 6101.04 and 6101.15 of the Revised Code to authorize conservancy districts to provide for the collection and disposal of solid waste.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Establish STEM degree loan repayment program/tax credit
description: To amend sections 5725.98, 5726.98, 5729.98, 5747.98, and 5751.98 and to enact sections 3333.27 and 3333.271 of the Revised Code to establish the STEM Degree Loan Repayment Program, to authorize a refundable tax credit for employers who make payments on student loans obtained by a graduate to earn a STEM degree, and to make an appropriation.

title: Change funerary laws
description: To amend sections 2108.75, 2108.82, 3705.20, 4513.17, 4717.04, 4717.05, 4717.07, 4717.10, 4717.13, 4717.14, 4717.28, 4717.30, 4717.31, 4717.35, and 4717.36 of the Revised Code to make changes to the laws that impact funeral homes, funeral professionals, funeral hearses, funeral escort vehicles, and preneed funeral contracts.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Designate US Army Specialist Christian D. Ward Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.872 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 60 in Washington County as the "U.S. Army Specialist Christian D. Ward Memorial Highway."

title: Designate Hispanic and Latino Heritage Month
description: To enact section 5.246 of the Revised Code to designate September 15 to October 15 each year as "Hispanic and Latino Heritage Month."
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Designate separate sports and teams for each sex at schools
description: To enact sections 3313.5317 and 3345.561 of the Revised Code to enact the "Save Women's Sports Act" to require schools, state institutions of higher education, and private colleges to designate separate single-sex teams and sports for each sex.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Amends law relating to insurers being credited for reinsurance
description: To amend sections 3901.62 and 3901.64 of the Revised Code to amend the law related to insurers receiving credit for reinsurance.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Insurance and Financial Institutions

title: Audit performance of schools with academic distress commissions
description: To require one-time performance audits of school districts with a current academic distress commission.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Regards state retirement system fiduciary duties/fees/pay
description: To amend section 145.11 and to enact sections 145.096, 145.117, 145.118, 742.117, 3307.155, 3309.151, and 5505.066 of the Revised Code regarding state retirement system fiduciary duties, Public Employees Retirement System management fees and employee pay, and creating the Committee on Pension Salaries and Fees.

title: Audit JobsOhio/include enterprise acquisition project
description: To amend sections 117.01, 117.10, 187.01, and 187.04 and to enact section 117.561 of the Revised Code to establish that JobsOhio must submit to audits by the Auditor of State, and that an audit of JobsOhio must include an audit of the revenues, receipts, and expenditures of JobsOhio associated with the enterprise acquisition project.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Adopt standards for mental health education
description: To amend sections 3301.0718 and 3313.60 of the Revised Code and to amend Sections 265.10 and 265.80 of H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to require the State Board of Education to adopt standards for mental health education, to permit school districts to include mental health education in their curriculum, and to make an appropriation.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Address women's policy and research
description: To amend section 121.30 and to enact sections 121.34, 121.341, and 121.342 of the Revised Code to create the Women's Policy and Research Commission and the Women's Policy and Research Center, to specify their powers and duties, and to make an appropriation.

title: Regards identifying information in workers' comp claims
description: To amend sections 4121.44, 4121.441, and 4121.442 of the Revised Code regarding identifying information in the medical management of workers' compensation claims.

title: Prohibit discrimination based on hair texture and styles
description: To amend sections 3314.03, 3326.11, 3328.24, and 4112.01 and to enact section 3319.48 of the Revised Code to enact the Creating a Respectful and Open World for Natural Hair (CROWN) Act to prohibit discrimination against an individual based on hair texture and protective hair styles.

title: Regards state benefit eligibility if relocate call center abroad
description: To enact sections 4113.87, 4113.88, 4113.89, 4113.90, 4113.91, and 4113.92 of the Revised Code to enact the Consumer Protection Call Center Act regarding the eligibility of employers that relocate a call center to a foreign country to receive state grants, loans, and other benefits.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Economic and Workforce Development

title: Create Fallen Timbers Battlefield license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.714 of the Revised Code to create the "Fallen Timbers Battlefield" license plate.

title: Prohibit abortions based upon condition precedent
description: To enact sections 2904.01, 2904.02, 2904.03, 2904.031, 2904.032, 2904.04, 2904.05, 2904.22, 2904.30, 2904.35, and 2904.40 of the Revised Code to enact the Human Life Protection Act to prohibit abortions based upon a condition precedent.

title: Permit township police to enforce traffic laws on interstates
description: To amend sections 4513.35 and 4513.39 of the Revised Code to permit a township police officer who serves a population between 5,000 and 50,000 to enforce specified traffic offenses on interstate highways within the township if authorized by that township's board of trustees.

title: Create automated voter registration and verification
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Regards valuation adjustments for destroyed or injured property
description: To amend section 319.38 of the Revised Code regarding valuation adjustments for destroyed or injured property.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Insurance and Financial Institutions

title: Revise OPFPF disability determination procedures
description: To amend section 742.38 of the Revised Code to revise Ohio Police and Fire Pension Fund disability determination procedures.

title: Regards Uniform Duties to Incapacitated Persons Act/identifiers
description: To amend sections 2305.41, 2305.42, 2305.43, 2305.44, 2305.45, 2305.48, and 2305.49 of the Revised Code regarding the Uniform Duties to Incapacitated Persons Act and identifying devices.

title: Creates Cuyahoga Valley Scenic Railroad license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.596 of the Revised Code to create the "Cuyahoga Valley Scenic Railroad" license plate.

title: Establish conditions for sale of brine as a commodity
description: To enact section 1509.228 of the Revised Code to establish conditions and requirements for the sale of brine from oil or gas operations as a commodity and to exempt that commodity from requirements otherwise applicable to brine.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Energy and Natural Resources

title: Alter definitions-hybrid/plug-in vehicles/halve registration fee
description: To amend sections 4501.01 and 4503.10 of the Revised Code to alter the definitions of "plug-in electric motor vehicle" and "hybrid motor vehicle" under the motor vehicle law and to halve the additional registration fees for those two types of motor vehicles.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Restrict cost sharing-occupational/physical therapists
description: To enact sections 3902.50 and 3902.51 of the Revised Code to restrict cost sharing requirements with regard to occupational and physical therapists.

title: Designate Sarcoidosis Awareness Month
description: To enact section 5.2521 of the Revised Code to designate April as "Sarcoidosis Awareness Month."
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Regards operation/accountability of community schools
description: To amend sections 117.11, 149.43, 3313.844, 3313.849, 3314.01, 3314.02, 3314.032, and 3314.05 and to enact sections 117.102, 3314.0111, 3314.0310, 3314.0311, and 3314.0312 of the Revised Code regarding the operation, management, and accountability of community schools.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Change motorcycle safety and education program
description: To amend sections 4508.08 and 4513.17 of the Revised Code to make changes to the laws related to the motorcycle safety and education program and to clarify that a motorcycle may have a modulating headlight that meets federal standards.
last_action: Reported - Amended: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Change laws relating to euthanasia of animals
description: To amend sections 959.06, 4729.01, 4729.531, 4729.532, and 4729.54 of the Revised Code to prohibit an animal shelter from using a gas chamber to euthanize an animal and to make changes to the law governing euthanasia of an animal by lethal injection.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Regards parole for offenders who committed offense when under 18
description: To amend sections 2907.02, 2909.24, 2929.02, 2929.03, 2929.06, 2929.14, 2929.19, 2967.13, 2971.03, and 5149.101 and to enact sections 2929.07 and 2967.132 of the Revised Code regarding a bar against a sentence of life without parole, and special parole dates, for offenders who committed the offense when under age 18.

title: Specify open hunting of coyotes
description: To amend sections 1533.02 and 1533.111 of the Revised Code to specify that a fur taker permit is not required to hunt or trap coyotes and to specify that there is no closed season for hunting or trapping coyotes.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Energy and Natural Resources

title: Authorize pregnant minor to consent to health care
description: To enact section 3701.62 of the Revised Code to authorize a pregnant minor to consent to receive health care to maintain or improve her life or the life of the unborn child she is carrying.

title: Change child support laws with regard to caretakers
Provide benefits for first responders with PTSD
description: To amend section 5502.01 and to enact sections 145.364, 742.391, 3309.402, 4168.01, 4168.02, 4168.09, 4168.10, 4168.11, 4168.12, 4168.20, 4168.21, 4168.45, 4168.46, 4168.47, 4168.48, 4168.60, 4168.75, 4168.76, 4168.77, 4168.78, 4168.80, and 5505.182 of the Revised Code concerning compensation, medical benefits, and disability retirement for peace officers, firefighters, and emergency medical workers diagnosed with post-traumatic stress disorder arising from employment without an accompanying physical injury.

title: Allow public bodies-video meetings during health emergency
description: To enact section 121.221 of the Revised Code to authorize public bodies to meet via teleconference and video conference during a public health state of emergency as declared by the Governor, and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Child abuse in military families-report to military authorities
description: To amend sections 2151.421 and 2151.423 and to enact section 2151.4210 of the Revised Code to require public children services agencies to report child abuse or neglect in military families to the appropriate military authorities.

title: Allow certain hunting/fishing without permit or free permit
description: To amend sections 1533.10, 1533.11, 1533.111, 1533.12, 1533.32, and 1533.81 of the Revised Code to generally allow the grandchildren of a landowner to hunt or fish on the landowner's property without obtaining certain hunting or fishing licenses or permits and to allow certain partially disabled veterans to receive free hunting or fishing licenses or permits.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Modify the law governing elections
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Suspend physician assistant practice requirements
description: To suspend the requirement that a physician assistant practice with supervision and to declare an emergency.

title: Prohibit eviction and foreclosure during COVID-19
description: To prohibit foreclosure activity and the eviction of residential and commercial tenants during the state of emergency declared regarding COVID-19, to refer such proceedings caused by the state of emergency to mediation, and to declare an emergency.

title: Require notice of EMT transport of contagious patient
description: To amend sections 149.43, 3701.17, and 3727.99 and to enact sections 3707.11, 3707.111, 3727.80, and 3727.801 of the Revised Code to require prompt notification of an emergency medical services worker who has treated or transported a patient who is presumptively positive for a contagious or infectious disease and to declare an emergency.

title: Prevent utility disruption during COVID-19
description: To prevent the disruption of utility service during the state of emergency declared regarding COVID-19 and to declare an emergency.

title: Extend income tax filing deadline
description: To extend the filing and payment dates for state, municipal, and school district income taxes by the same period as any federal income tax extension granted in response to the COVID-19 disease outbreak and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Ways and Means

title: Increase local government fund distribution
description: To amend Section 387.20 of H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to increase the percentage of revenue to the General Revenue Fund distributed to the Local Government Fund and to declare an emergency.

title: Authorize partially refundable earned income tax credit
description: To temporarily authorize a partially refundable earned income tax credit and to declare an emergency.

title: Require insurance and medicaid coverage of certain drugs
description: To amend section 5167.12 and to enact sections 3902.50, 3902.51, and 5164.7516 of the Revised Code to require insurance and Medicaid coverage of a ninety-day supply of certain covered drugs and to declare an emergency.

title: Extend publicly funded childcare rating deadline
description: To amend section 5104.31 of the Revised Code to extend the date by which publicly funded child care providers must be rated through the Step Up to Quality Program and to declare an emergency.

title: Revise open bidding during COVID-19 emergency
description: To allow a county to purchase public health-related items and communication equipment without competitive bidding, solicit bids electronically, and open bids during a meeting held electronically during the period of the emergency declared by Executive Order 2020-01D, issued on March 9, 2020, and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Make COVID-19 an occupational disease in some cases
description: To amend section 4123.68 of the Revised Code to make COVID-19 contracted by a peace officer, firefighter, or emergency medical worker an occupational disease under the Workers' Compensation Law under certain circumstances and to declare an emergency.

title: Allow certain offices to waive penalties and fees
description: To allow the Ohio Public Works Commission, the Ohio Water Development Authority, the Ohio Environmental Protection Agency, county auditors, and county recorders, during the state of emergency due to COVID-19, to waive certain penalties and late fees, suspend certain reporting requirements, and waive electronic recording fees, and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Make COVID-19 an occupational disease in some cases
description: To amend section 4123.68 of the Revised Code to make COVID-19 an occupational disease under the Workers' Compensation Law under certain circumstances and to declare an emergency.

title: Transfer and appropriate money for unemployment comp
description: To transfer moneys to the Unemployment Compensation Special Administration Fund, to make an appropriation, and to declare an emergency.

title: Permit county payments by drop box during COVID-19
description: To permit counties to receive payments by a drop box instead of in-person for the duration of the Governor's COVID-19 emergency declaration and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Suspend homestead interest penalty and foreclosure
description: To temporarily abate the charging of interest and penalties against tax-delinquent homesteads and suspend tax foreclosure proceedings and tax certificate sales regarding such homesteads and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Ways and Means

title: Dissolve academic distress commissions and create new board
description: To amend sections 3302.036 and 3302.17; to enact new sections 3302.101 and 3302.102 and sections 3301.28, 3301.29, and 3302.103; and to repeal sections 3302.042, 3302.101, 3302.102, and 3302.12 of the Revised Code and to repeal Section 265.520 of H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to dissolve existing academic distress commissions, to place a moratorium on the creation of academic distress commissions, to establish the School Transformation Board, and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Support homeless shelters and rent assistance due to COVID-19
description: To make an appropriation to support homeless shelters in the state and to provide emergency rental assistance in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, and to declare an emergency.

title: Require health plan issuers cover COVID-19 test and treatment
description: To enact sections 3902.50, 3902.51, 3902.52, and 3902.53 of the Revised Code to require health insurers to cover COVID-19 testing and treatment, to prohibit balance billing, to make an appropriation, and to declare an emergency.

title: Require health plan issuers cover telemedicine in emergency
description: To amend section 3902.30 of the Revised Code to require health plan issuers to cover telemedicine services during a state of emergency, and to declare an emergency.

title: Address immediate concerns related to COVID-19
description: To address immediate concerns related to COVID-19.

title: Makes appropriations and capital reappropriations
description: To make appropriations for the biennium ending June 30, 2021, and capital reappropriations for the biennium ending June 30, 2022.

title: Temporarily waive certain Medicaid requirements
description: To waive certain Medicaid requirements during the COVID-19 emergency declaration and to declare an emergency.

title: Temporarily change unemployment eligibility requirements
description: To temporarily change eligibility and work search requirements under the Unemployment Compensation Law and to declare an emergency.

title: Waive K-12 requirements due to COVID-19 closures
description: To waive certain primary and secondary education requirements to account for school closings in compliance with the Director of Health's order due to the implications of COVID-19 and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Revise government procurement requirements
description: To amend sections 125.035 and 125.607 of the Revised Code to require any state agency seeking to purchase supplies or services to comply with an applicable first or second procurement program and to prohibit any governmental ordering office from combining multiple parts of a larger related project to avoid using the procurement list maintained by community rehabilitation programs.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Suspend and revise Ed Choice Scholarships
description: To amend sections 3310.02, 3310.03, 3310.032, 3310.08, and 3310.16 of the Revised Code to suspend all performance-based Educational Choice scholarships for the 2020-2021 school year, to make changes to the Educational Choice Scholarship Program and the expansion of that program for the 2021-2022 school year and each school year thereafter, and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Temporarily modify public assistance requirements
description: To delay eligibility redeterminations for certain public assistance programs, temporarily modify income eligibility requirements for certain public assistance programs, and to declare an emergency.

title: Require certain insurance cover pandemic losses
description: To require insurers offering business interruption insurance to cover losses attributable to viruses and pandemics and to declare an emergency.

title: Prohibit price gouging after a declaration of emergency
description: To amend section 1345.99 and to enact section 1345.033 of the Revised Code to prohibit price gouging after a declaration of an emergency and to declare an emergency.

title: Suspend municipal income tax withholding while COVID-19
description: To suspend some employer municipal income tax withholding requirements during the COVID-19 state of emergency and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Ways and Means

title: Appropriate money to Association of Community Health Centers
description: To make an appropriation for the Ohio Association of Community Health Centers and to declare an emergency.

title: Provide paid leave to quarantined/compensate contract workers
description: To enact sections 4143.01, 4143.02, 4143.03, 4143.04, 4143.05, 4143.06, 4143.07, 4143.08, 4143.09, and 4143.99 of the Revised Code to require paid leave for an employee who is unable to work due to quarantine or mandatory isolation, to create a grant program to compensate contract workers who cannot perform services during public health emergencies, to make an appropriation, and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Employ retired first responders
description: To amend sections 742.44 and 5505.51 and to enact sections 145.386, 742.261, 3307.355, and 3309.346 of the Revised Code regarding re-employment of a retirant as a police officer, firefighter, or emergency medical worker during a state of emergency, to allow a deferred retirement option plan participant to work past the participant's employment end date during a state of emergency, and to declare an emergency.

title: Release college transcript regardless of student debt
description: To enact section 3345.60 of the Revised Code to require institutions of higher education to release student transcripts regardless of student-owed institutional debt.

title: Halt debt collection
description: To halt the collection of debts and to declare an emergency.

title: Halt debt collection/freeze interest against state universities
description: To halt the collection of all debt owed to any state institution of higher education or hospital operated by a state institution of higher education, to freeze the accrual of interest and collection of fees on all outstanding debt owed to those entities, and to declare an emergency.

title: Authorize EMTs to perform medical services in hospitals
description: To temporarily authorize emergency medical technicians to perform certain medical services in hospitals and to declare an emergency.

title: Notify medical/funeral workers if handle COVID-19 patient
description: To require, during the period of the emergency declared by Executive Order 2020-01D issued on March 9, 2020, and for six months thereafter, prompt notification of an emergency medical services worker or funeral services worker who has treated, handled, or transported a patient who tested positive for COVID-19 and to declare an emergency.

title: Authorize commercial driver licensee to drive ambulance
description: To authorize a person who has had a valid commercial driver's license to drive an ambulance during the state of emergency due to COVID-19 and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Require public restrooms to have adult changing station
description: To amend sections 3781.109 and 5747.98 and to enact section 5747.79 of the Revised Code to enact Matthew's Law, requiring public buildings to have at least one rest room facility with an adult changing station and authorizing an income tax credit for installation.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Modify publication of property tax foreclosures
description: To amend sections 323.25, 323.69, 5721.14, and 5721.18 of the Revised Code to modify the manner in which property tax foreclosure notices may be published.

title: Relieve student loan payments/study how assist higher ed COVID-19
description: To require the Chancellor of Higher Education to waive interest rates and suspend payments on state student loan programs for up to sixty days, to require the Chancellor to conduct a study to assist institutions of higher education and students during the COVID-19 outbreak, and to declare an emergency.

title: Regards criminal records sealing law
Cover under Workers Comp COVID-19 contracted by food worker
description: To amend section 4123.68 of the Revised Code to make COVID-19 contracted by an employee of a retail food establishment or food processing establishment an occupational disease under the Workers' Compensation Law under certain circumstances and to declare an emergency.

title: Grant immunity to essential workers who transmit COVID-19
description: To make temporary changes related to qualified civil immunity for health care and emergency services provided during a government-declared disaster or emergency and for exposure to or transmission or contraction of certain coronaviruses.

title: Create Gender Pay Disparity Task Force
description: To create the Gender Pay Disparity Task Force.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Change peace officer/firefighter certification during COVID-19
description: To make changes to the law regarding peace officer and firefighter certifications during the declaration of an emergency regarding COVID-19 and to declare an emergency.
last_action: Refer to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Administer a temporary tax amnesty program

title: To delegate to the President of the Senate and the Speaker of the House of Representatives authority to designate groups of members to prepare arguments for and against amendments to the Ohio Constitution proposed by the General Assembly, a person or persons to prepare an argument for any law, section, or item submitted to the electors by referendum petition, and a person or persons to prepare an argument against any constitutional amendment proposed by initiative petition.

title: Recognize Jerusalem as the capital of Israel
description: To recognize Jerusalem as the capital of the State of Israel.

description: To urge the Congress of the United States to grant additional authority to the Federal Communications Commission to stop unwanted robocalls and "call spoofing" and to urge the Commission to educate the public on how to report illegal telephone calls.
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: Public Utilities

description: To encourage equitable and diverse gender representation on the boards and in senior management of Ohio companies and institutions.

title: Urge Congress to prohibit internet cut off during COVID-19.
description: To urge the Congress and the President of the United States to prohibit internet service providers from terminating internet service to residential customers and small businesses during the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Urge Congress-designate Juneteenth Independence Day
description: To urge the United States Congress to pass and the President of the United States to sign legislation to designate June 19th as a national holiday, to be known as Juneteenth Independence Day, in recognition of June 19, 1865, the date on which news of the end of slavery reached enslaved people in the southwestern states.
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: State and Local Government

description: Proposing to amend Section 1 of Article V of the Constitution of the State of Ohio to permit an elector to register to vote on Election Day and vote in that election.

description: Proposing to amend Section 6 of Article I of the Constitution of the State of Ohio to prohibit slavery or involuntary servitude in Ohio for the punishment of crime.

title: Reduce number of regulatory restrictions

description: To amend sections 939.02, 940.06, 6119.06, 6119.09, and 6119.091 and to enact sections 940.36 and 940.37 of the Revised Code to create the Statewide Watershed Planning and Management Program under the administration of the Director of Agriculture and to make changes to the law governing regional water and sewer districts.

title: Express intent to reform drug sentencing laws
Make capital appropriations for school facilities assistance
description: To amend sections 307.86, 339.05, 505.08, 731.14, 749.37, and 5540.03 of the Revised Code to apply the Prevailing Wage Law to transportation improvement district projects under certain circumstances, to temporarily expand the use of certain tax increment financing payments, to exempt personal protective equipment from certain political subdivision competitive bidding law during the period of the emergency declared by Executive Order 2020-01D, issued on March 9, 2020, and to make capital appropriations to the Facilities Construction Commission and the Public Works Commission for the biennium ending June 30, 2022.

title: Regulate temporary child hosting with host families

title: Regards temporary occupational licensing for military
description: To amend sections 4743.04 and 5903.04 and to enact section 4743.041 of the Revised Code to require state occupational licensing agencies, under certain circumstances, to issue temporary licenses or certificates to members of the military and spouses who are licensed in another jurisdiction and have moved to Ohio for military duty.

title: Authorize tax credit for investment in opportunity zone
description: To amend sections 107.036, 122.86, 5747.02, and 5747.98 and to enact section 122.84 of the Revised Code to authorize a tax credit for investments in an Ohio Opportunity Zone.

description: To amend section 3904.13 and to enact section 3901.89 of the Revised Code to require health plan issuers to release certain claim information to group plan policyholders.

last_action: Effective 1/7/21 - Emergency: Act effective January 7 2021; certain provisions effective April 7, 2021

title: Adds protections/mediation to civil rights law/keeps exemption

description: To establish the Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias Task Force.

last_action: Refer to Committee: Health, Human Services and Medicaid

title: Regards Medicaid copayment requirements

last_action: Refer to Committee: Health, Human Services and Medicaid

title: Create the Women's Suffrage Centennial Commission
description: To create the Women's Suffrage Centennial Commission.

last_action: Conference managers: Sens. - Eklund, Roegner, Thomas

title: Revise law governing pet cemeteries

committee: Senate Local Government Public Safety and Veterans Affairs
last_action: Refer to Committee: Local Government, Public Safety and Veterans Affairs

title: Regards school employment, educator licensure, and conduct
last_action: Refer to Committee: Primary and Secondary Education

description: To amend sections 107.036, 122.85, 5726.98, 5733.98, 5747.98, and 5751.98 of the Revised Code to extend eligibility for and make other changes to the motion picture tax credit.

title: Revise municipal use of water and sewer funds
description: To amend sections 729.52 and 743.06 of the Revised Code to revise the law governing municipal corporation use of water and sewer funds.
committee: Senate Local Government Public Safety and Veterans Affairs
last_action: Refer to Committee: Local Government, Public Safety and Veterans Affairs

title: Authorize insurance premiums tax credit for certain projects
description: To amend sections 107.036, 1311.87, 1311.88, 1311.90, 5725.98, 5729.98, and 5747.98 and to enact sections 122.09, 5725.35, 5729.18, and 5747.29 of the Revised Code to authorize an insurance premiums tax credit for capital contributions to transformational mixed use development projects, to authorize an income tax credit for campaign contributions, to modify the law governing commercial real estate broker liens, and to declare an emergency.

description: To amend sections 3505.03, 3505.04, and 3513.13 of the Revised Code to require uncontested races to appear at the end of an election ballot.

description: To amend sections 2945.71 and 2945.73 of the Revised Code to narrow the scope of speedy trial rules.

last_action: Refer to Committee: General Government and Agency Review

title: Denounce discriminatory words and deeds resulting from COVID-19
description: To denounce all forms of stigmatization, racism, and discrimination occurring as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Require branches of government conform to prescribed authority
description: To resolve that no branch of government, be it executive, legislative, or judicial, nor any subordinate department, agency, or entity thereof, may take or otherwise promulgate actions that exceed its authority to do so as expressly granted under the Ohio Constitution or United States Constitution.
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: State and Local Government

title: Declare racism a public health crisis/promote racial equality
description: To declare racism a public health crisis and to ask the Governor to establish a working group to promote racial equity in Ohio.
committee: Senate Health, Human Services and Medicaid
last_action: Refer to Committee: Health, Human Services and Medicaid

title: Locate US Space Command HQ in Ohio
description: To urge the United States government to select Ohio to host the permanent headquarters of the United States Space Command.

title: Remedy police use of excess force/oppose defunding police
description: To call for justice for victims of excessive force by police and declare opposition to efforts to defund law enforcement.
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: Transportation and Public Safety

title: Encourage corporations and unions to disclose political donors
description: To urge Congress to pass legislation requiring corporations and labor organizations that make political expenditures to disclose the identities of their donors.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Government Oversight and Reform

title: Make Pete Rose eligible for baseball Hall of Fame
description: To urge the Commissioner of Major League Baseball to remove Peter Edward "Pete" Rose from Major League Baseball's permanently ineligible list as soon as possible, and to urge the Baseball Writers' Association of America and the National Baseball Hall of Fame to include Rose on the Hall of Fame ballot.
committee: Senate General Government and Agency Review
last_action: Refer to Committee: General Government and Agency Review

title: Issue bonds for clean water improvements
description: Proposing to enact Section 2t of Article VIII of the Constitution of the State of Ohio to permit the issuance of general obligation bonds to fund clean water improvements.

title: Apply for US Constitutional Convention-limit federal government
description: Application to the Congress of the United States for a Convention of the States under Article V of the Constitution of the United States that is limited to proposing amendments that impose fiscal restraints on the federal government, limit the power and jurisdiction of the federal government, and limit the terms of office for its officials and Members of Congress.
committee: Senate General Government and Agency Review
last_action: Refer to Committee: General Government and Agency Review

title: Approve income tax increases by supermajority
description: Proposing to enact Section 7 of Article XII of the Constitution of the State of Ohio to require that any increase in income tax rates be approved by a supermajority of the membership of each house of the General Assembly.

title: Issue bonds to repay unemployment compensation advances
description: Proposing to enact Section 18 of Article VIII of the Constitution of the State of Ohio to allow the General Assembly to provide for the issuance of obligations to repay outstanding advances made by the federal government to the unemployment compensation program of the state.

title: Removes slavery/involuntary servitude as punishment for crime
description: Proposing to amend Section 6 of Article I of the Constitution of the State of Ohio to prohibit slavery or involuntary servitude in Ohio for the punishment of crime.
last_action: Refer to Committee: Government Oversight and Reform
